title: Quiz 9
subtitle: B1 Daily Skills Challenge
source: Original B1 quiz created for this system

questions.question: I _____ this film before, so I know the ending.
questions.explanation: Use present perfect for a life experience when the exact time is not important.

questions.question: We _____ the museum last Saturday.
questions.explanation: Use past simple with a finished time expression like last Saturday.

questions.question: Maya has lived in this town _____ 2021.
questions.explanation: Use since with the starting point of a period of time.

questions.question: They have known each other _____ five years.
questions.explanation: Use for with a length of time.

questions.question: I _____ dinner when the phone rang.
questions.explanation: Use past continuous for the longer action in progress when another action happened.

questions.question: When I was younger, I _____ afraid of big dogs.
questions.explanation: Used to describes a past state or habit that is not true now.

questions.question: We _____ visit Grandma this weekend. We bought the train tickets yesterday.
questions.explanation: Be going to is used for plans and intentions, especially when they are already decided.

questions.question: That bag looks heavy. I _____ help you carry it.
questions.explanation: Will is often used for a decision made at the moment of speaking.

questions.question: If it _____ tomorrow, we will play board games inside.
questions.explanation: In the first conditional, use present simple after if and will in the result.

questions.question: You cannot enter the room _____ you have a key.
questions.explanation: Unless means if not.

questions.question: Students _____ wear sports shoes in the gym.
questions.explanation: Must shows a rule or strong obligation.

questions.question: You look tired. You _____ go to bed earlier tonight.
questions.explanation: Should is used to give advice.

questions.question: Chocolate _____ from cocoa beans.
questions.explanation: Present simple passive uses am/is/are + past participle.

questions.question: The school prize _____ by Liam last year.
questions.explanation: Past simple passive uses was/were + past participle.

questions.question: The girl _____ lives next door is in my class.
questions.explanation: Use who for people in relative clauses.

questions.question: This is the cafe _____ we met after school.
questions.explanation: Use where for places in relative clauses.

questions.question: This puzzle is _____ than the one we did yesterday.
questions.explanation: Use comparative adjectives with than.

questions.question: That was _____ story in the whole book.
questions.explanation: Use the + superlative when comparing one thing with all the others in a group.

questions.question: The backpack is not big _____ for all my books.
questions.explanation: Enough comes after an adjective: big enough.

questions.question: There are _____ biscuits left on the plate.
questions.explanation: Use a few with plural countable nouns like biscuits.

questions.question: My cousin can play _____ violin.
questions.explanation: Use the before many musical instruments: play the violin.

questions.question: Noah is interested _____ space and planets.
questions.explanation: The adjective interested is followed by in.

questions.question: Can you _____ my cat while I am on holiday?
questions.explanation: Look after means take care of.

questions.question: We need to _____ what time the movie starts.
questions.explanation: Find out means discover information.

questions.question: The maths problem was hard, but Erin did not _____.
questions.explanation: Give up means stop trying.

questions.question: I have to _____ my homework before dinner.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is do homework.

questions.question: After studying for an hour, we decided to _____ a short break.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is take a break.

questions.question: The children's _____ filled the room after the game. (happy)
questions.explanation: Happy is an adjective. Happiness is the noun.

questions.question: Be _____ when you carry the hot soup. (care)
questions.explanation: Care is a noun or verb. Careful is the adjective meaning not careless.

questions.question: This website is very _____ when I study English. (use)
questions.explanation: Use is a verb or noun. Useful is the adjective.

questions.question: Ravi ran _____ to catch the bus. (quick)
questions.explanation: Quick is an adjective. Quickly is the adverb.

questions.question: The artist showed great _____ in her painting. (imagine)
questions.explanation: Imagine is a verb. Imagination is the noun.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: I have lived here for three years
questions.explanation: Use present perfect with for to show a situation that started in the past and continues now.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: She used to play tennis after school
questions.explanation: Used to + base verb describes a past habit.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: If it rains we will stay at home
questions.explanation: First conditional uses if + present simple, will + base verb.

questions.reading: Every Thursday, a small group of students meets behind the library to repair old bikes. At first, the club only fixed bikes for students at school. Later, they began collecting broken bikes from neighbours and giving the repaired bikes to younger children. The club leader, Hana, says the best part is not the tools or the bikes, but watching someone ride away with a huge smile.
questions.question: Where does the bike repair club meet?
questions.explanation: The text says the group meets behind the library.

questions.reading: Every Thursday, a small group of students meets behind the library to repair old bikes. At first, the club only fixed bikes for students at school. Later, they began collecting broken bikes from neighbours and giving the repaired bikes to younger children. The club leader, Hana, says the best part is not the tools or the bikes, but watching someone ride away with a huge smile.
questions.question: What did the club start doing later?
questions.explanation: Later, they collected broken bikes from neighbours and gave repaired bikes to younger children.

questions.reading: Every Thursday, a small group of students meets behind the library to repair old bikes. At first, the club only fixed bikes for students at school. Later, they began collecting broken bikes from neighbours and giving the repaired bikes to younger children. The club leader, Hana, says the best part is not the tools or the bikes, but watching someone ride away with a huge smile.
questions.question: What does Hana probably enjoy most?
questions.explanation: She enjoys helping people, because she loves seeing someone ride away happily.

questions.reading: Leo volunteered as a junior guide at the city museum. He was nervous before his first tour because he thought visitors might ask difficult questions. His supervisor told him to be honest if he did not know an answer and to offer to find out. During the tour, one visitor asked about a painting Leo had never seen before. Leo followed the advice, checked the information desk, and returned with the answer.
questions.question: Why was Leo nervous before his first tour?
questions.explanation: He worried that visitors might ask difficult questions.
options: He thought visitors might ask difficult questions
He had never been to the museum

questions.reading: Leo volunteered as a junior guide at the city museum. He was nervous before his first tour because he thought visitors might ask difficult questions. His supervisor told him to be honest if he did not know an answer and to offer to find out. During the tour, one visitor asked about a painting Leo had never seen before. Leo followed the advice, checked the information desk, and returned with the answer.
questions.question: What did Leo do when he did not know the answer?
questions.explanation: He checked the information desk and returned with the answer.

questions.reading: Leo volunteered as a junior guide at the city museum. He was nervous before his first tour because he thought visitors might ask difficult questions. His supervisor told him to be honest if he did not know an answer and to offer to find out. During the tour, one visitor asked about a painting Leo had never seen before. Leo followed the advice, checked the information desk, and returned with the answer.
questions.question: What lesson did Leo learn?
questions.explanation: He learned that it is better to be honest and find the answer than to pretend.
options: It is okay to be honest and find out

questions.question: Tom said that he _____ busy the next day.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, am/is often changes to was after a past reporting verb.

questions.question: My brother enjoys _____ games with his friends.
questions.explanation: Enjoy is followed by verb-ing.

questions.question: We decided _____ a different route home.
questions.explanation: Decide is followed by to + base verb.

questions.question: _____ it was raining, we still enjoyed the walk.
questions.explanation: Although introduces a contrast between two ideas.

questions.question: The bus was late _____ the heavy traffic.
questions.explanation: Because of is followed by a noun phrase, such as the heavy traffic.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Information is uncountable, so do not add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Present perfect uses have/has + past participle: have finished.
options: I finished my work since Monday.

questions.question: The instructions were clear and easy to follow. They were very _____. (help)
questions.explanation: Help is a verb or noun. Helpful is the adjective.

questions.question: A good _____ can make a difficult project easier. (friend)
questions.explanation: Friend is a person. Friendship is the relationship between friends.
